rulings.Case Title: LEILA STRINGFELLOW, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR AND AS et al VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Union Pacific Railroad Company To Compel The Depositions Of Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Union Pacific Railroad Company's motion to compel the depositions of plaintiffs Mary Jean Wozny, Marlene Warlick and Rebecca Cummings is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY WILLIAMS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 75, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order (1) Compelling Defendant B.E. Development Corporation To Provide Further Substantive Responses To Plaintiffs Discovery, Including Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Production, And Requests For Admission; And (2) For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for an order (1) compelling defendant B.E. Development Corporation to provide further substantive responses to plaintiffs' discovery, including special interrogatories, form interrogatories, requests for production and requests for admission; and (2) for monetary sanctions is continued on the court's own motion to June 18, 2014 due to the court's schedule. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY WILLIAMS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 75, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order (1) Compelling Defendant Viking Pump, Inc. To Provide Further Substantive Responses To Plaintiffs Discovery, Including Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Production, And Requests For Admission; And (2) For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for an order (1) compelling defendant Viking Pump, Inc. to provide further substantive responses to plaintiffs' discovery, including special interrogatories, form interrogatories, requests for production and requests for admission; and (2) for monetary sanctions is off calendar due to an apparent clerical mistake. It appears plaintiffs entered the wrong defendant's name in the Notice of Motion and that plaintiffs meant to file this motion as to defendant B.E. Development Corp., against whom plaintiffs also have a motion to compel set for hearing on June 10, 2014. If a hearing is requested to contest this motion being taken off calendar, it will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Ford Motor Co.S Further Compliance With Plaintiffs Amended Notice Of Deposition Of Pmq-Cor
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Plaintiff's motion to compel defendant Ford Motor Company's further compliance with plaintiffs' amended notice of deposition of person(s) most qualified and custodian(s) of records: Plaintiffs' motion to compel defendant Kelsey-Hayes Company's further responses to plaintiffs' PMQ/COR notice of deposition: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. The Court will determine if sanctions will be imposed on the appropriate party. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
